description: Assembly review of 5 knowledge graph batches (batch-0 through batch-4) covering 28 JS files of Mineradio music player. Two distinct ID schemes: batch 0 uses qualified IDs (file:/function:/data: prefixes), batches 1-4 use flat unqualified IDs. Cross-batch edges rely on flat IDs and resolve correctly across batches 1-4. Batch 0 edges are self-contained within its own qualified namespace.
warnings: ID SCHEME MISMATCH: Batch 0 uses qualified IDs (e.g. 'file:public/js/api-helper.js', 'function:public/js/shared.js:songProviderKey') while batches 1-4 use flat IDs (e.g. 'api-helper.js', 'songProviderKey'). These are structurally distinct IDs → no literal collisions, but the same conceptual entities have duplicate representations.

FILE NODE DUPLICATION: 4 physical files appear in both batch 0 (qualified) and later batches (flat): public/js/api-helper.js (batch 0+1), public/js/login.js (batch 0+4), public/js/playlists.js (batch 0+4), public/js/core/three-lyrics.js (batch 0+3). Each should be merged into one node during assembly.

FUNCTION DUPLICATION: 5 function/data nodes have both a qualified ID in batch 0 and a flat ID in later batches: songProviderKey (batch 0+1), fxDefaults (batch 0+4), applyDiyMode (batch 0+4), openGsapModal (batch 0+4), closeGsapModal (batch 0+4). These are the same functions — will need deduplication during assembly.

DANGLING EDGE SOURCES/TARGETS: 26 dangling references detected across batches 1-4 (no matching node ID exists in any batch). These reference functions/filenames that were not captured as nodes in any batch.

CROSS-BATCH EDGE HOPPING: All edges from batch 0 stay within the qualified ID namespace. All edges from batches 1-4 use flat IDs and freely reference nodes across batches 1-4 (e.g. batch 2 edges target batch 1 nodes). This is functional but means the graph is split into two weakly-connected subgraphs via the ID scheme boundary.
